questionText: The AK-47 is exclusively available to the Terrorist side.
explanation: The AK-47 is a signature rifle for the Terrorist team in Counter-Strike 2.

questionText: Dust II is one of the original maps included in Counter-Strike 2's launch map pool.
explanation: Dust II, a classic map, was part of the initial competitive map pool for CS2.

questionText: A player can purchase a Defuse Kit on the Terrorist side.
explanation: Defuse Kits are only available to the Counter-Terrorist side to speed up bomb defusal.

questionText: The AWP is a one-shot kill to the body at any range if the opponent has no armor.
explanation: The AWP is an extremely powerful sniper rifle capable of eliminating unarmored opponents with a single body shot.

questionText: In CS2, players start with a P250 pistol in competitive matches.
explanation: Terrorists start with a Glock-18 and Counter-Terrorists start with a USP-S (or P2000) in competitive CS2.

questionText: A smoke grenade can extinguish a Molotov fire in CS2.
explanation: Deploying a smoke grenade on a Molotov fire will effectively put out the flames in CS2.

questionText: The primary objective of the Terrorist side is always to plant the bomb.
explanation: Terrorists can also win a round by eliminating all Counter-Terrorist players before the timer runs out.

questionText: Mirage has an 'Underpass' callout.
explanation: Underpass is a common callout located beneath the Mid area of the Mirage map.

questionText: s1mple is primarily known as an AWPer.
explanation: Oleksandr 's1mple' Kostyliev is widely recognized as one of the best AWPers in CS:GO and CS2 history.

questionText: Astralis is a Danish esports organization.
explanation: Astralis is a prominent esports organization based in Denmark, famous for its dominant CS:GO teams.

questionText: The buy menu can be accessed using the 'B' key by default.
explanation: Pressing 'B' is the standard keybind to open the buy menu during the buy phase of a round.

questionText: Headshots with a pistol always deal more damage than body shots.
explanation: Headshots are critical hits in CS2 and always deal significantly more damage than shots to the body or limbs.

questionText: Vertigo is set on top of a skyscraper.
explanation: The map Vertigo features bomb sites and combat areas located on the upper floors and rooftop of a towering skyscraper.

questionText: An Incendiary Grenade is the CT equivalent of a Molotov.
explanation: Both Molotovs (T-side) and Incendiary Grenades (CT-side) create a spreading fire zone that deals damage over time.

questionText: The 'eco round' strategy involves buying expensive weapons to save money.
explanation: An eco round strategy involves buying minimal or no equipment to save money for a full buy in subsequent rounds.

questionText: Flashbangs temporarily blind all players who are looking at them when they explode.
explanation: Flashbangs are designed to temporarily blind and deafen players who are facing their explosion.

questionText: The CT side wins a round if the bomb is successfully defused.
explanation: Defusing the planted bomb is one of the primary win conditions for the Counter-Terrorist team.

questionText: Nuke features two bomb sites, one above the other.
explanation: Nuke is unique for having its A site on the upper level and B site on the lower level of the facility.

questionText: The M4A4 has a silencer attached by default.
explanation: The M4A1-S is the silenced variant, while the M4A4 does not come with a silencer.

questionText: In CS2, utility grenades can be dropped for teammates.
explanation: Players can drop grenades (HE, smoke, flash, molotov/incendiary) for teammates to pick up and use.

questionText: FaZe Clan won the PGL Major Antwerp 2022.
explanation: FaZe Clan secured their first Major title by defeating NaVi in the grand final of PGL Major Antwerp 2022.

questionText: The Glock-18 is a Terrorist-exclusive pistol.
explanation: The Glock-18 is the default starting pistol for the Terrorist side in CS2.

questionText: The Desert Eagle is a high-damage, low-ammo pistol.
explanation: The Desert Eagle is known for its powerful shots and low magazine capacity, making it a high-skill weapon.

questionText: A player can hear the bomb defuse sound from anywhere on the map.
explanation: The bomb defuse sound is only audible to players within a certain range of the planted bomb.

questionText: Inferno features a location known as 'Banana'.
explanation: Banana is a famous curved pathway on Inferno that leads from the T spawn to the B bomb site.

questionText: The maximum number of players on a standard competitive team is five.
explanation: Competitive CS2 matches are played between two teams of five players each.

questionText: The first person to pick up the bomb on the Terrorist side automatically equips it.
explanation: The bomb is an inventory item and must be manually selected to be held or planted.

questionText: The Zeus x27 is a one-hit kill weapon at close range.
explanation: The Zeus x27, a taser, instantly eliminates an enemy at very close proximity.

questionText: The Overpass map features a canal area.
explanation: Overpass is known for its distinct water canal area, often a point of contention near the B site.

questionText: A player can survive a headshot from an AWP if they have full armor and health.
explanation: An AWP headshot is an instant kill regardless of armor or health, making it the most lethal shot in the game.

questionText: The default knife can be dropped for a teammate.
explanation: The default knife is a permanent item and cannot be dropped or traded with teammates.

questionText: Grenades thrown into water will make a splash sound.
explanation: Throwing a grenade into a body of water, such as on Overpass, will produce a distinct splash sound.

questionText: Natus Vincere (NaVi) is known for their strong performance in the CS:GO era.
explanation: NaVi achieved significant success in CS:GO, including winning the PGL Major Stockholm 2021.

questionText: The maximum amount of money a player can have in CS2 is $16,000.
explanation: Players cannot accumulate more than $16,000 in their in-game bank balance.

questionText: The map Ancient is characterized by its bright, open spaces and modern architecture.
explanation: Ancient is known for its ancient ruins, stone structures, and more enclosed, jungle-like areas.

questionText: The M4A1-S has a lower fire rate than the M4A4.
explanation: The M4A1-S, the silenced M4 variant, has a slightly slower rate of fire compared to the M4A4.

questionText: In CS2, the smoke grenade volume is dynamic and expands differently based on terrain.
explanation: CS2's volumetric smokes mean they react to their environment, flowing around objects and expanding more realistically.

questionText: The Terrorist side receives a bonus for planting the bomb, even if they lose the round.
explanation: Terrorists get a $300 bonus for planting the bomb, which they keep even if the round is lost.

questionText: The MAC-10 is a Terrorist-exclusive SMG.
explanation: The MAC-10 is one of the SMGs exclusively available for purchase by the Terrorist team.

questionText: The maximum number of utility grenades a player can carry is three.
explanation: A player can carry one HE, one smoke, one molotov/incendiary, and two flashbangs, totaling five utility items.

questionText: The SSG 08 (Scout) can one-shot kill an unarmored opponent to the chest.
explanation: The SSG 08 is capable of a one-shot kill to the chest against enemies without armor.

questionText: A full eco round typically involves buying only pistols or nothing at all.
explanation: An eco round minimizes spending to save money, often resulting in players only buying cheap pistols or saving all their funds.

questionText: The default round time limit in competitive CS2 is 2 minutes.
explanation: The standard competitive round time in CS2 is 1 minute and 55 seconds.

questionText: The map Train features two bomb sites located on train cars.
explanation: On Train, the bomb sites are located within the train yard, not directly on moving train cars.

questionText: ZywOo plays for Team Vitality.
explanation: Mathieu 'ZywOo' Herbaut is the star AWPer and rifler for the French esports organization Team Vitality.

questionText: The P90 has a high rate of fire and a large magazine capacity.
explanation: The P90 is known for its fast firing speed and generous 50-round magazine, making it effective in close-quarters combat.

questionText: The bonus for a CT defusing the bomb is higher than the bonus for a T planting the bomb.
explanation: Both defusing the bomb as a CT and planting the bomb as a T grant a $300 individual bonus.

questionText: In CS2, players can pick up dropped weapons by walking over them.
explanation: Walking over a dropped weapon will automatically pick it up, replacing the currently held weapon if a slot is occupied.

questionText: The MP9 is a CT-exclusive SMG.
explanation: The MP9 is an SMG that can only be purchased by the Counter-Terrorist team.

questionText: Inferno's 'Mid' area connects directly to both bomb sites.
explanation: Inferno's Mid connects to A site via Apartments and to B site via Arch, but not directly into the B site itself.

questionText: Device has won multiple Major titles with Astralis.
explanation: Nicolai 'device' Reedtz was a key player in Astralis's dominant era, winning four CS:GO Major championships.

questionText: The price of an AWP is $4750.
explanation: The AWP is one of the most expensive weapons in the game, costing $4750.

questionText: The loss bonus system resets immediately after a team wins a round.
explanation: The loss bonus system reduces by one stage after a team wins, rather than fully resetting to zero bonus.

questionText: The jump throw bind is no longer necessary in CS2 for consistent grenade throws.
explanation: CS2 introduced a built-in feature where holding both mouse buttons while throwing a grenade performs a consistent jump-throw.

questionText: The AUG is a CT-exclusive rifle.
explanation: The AUG is a scoped rifle that is only available for purchase by the Counter-Terrorist team.

questionText: The map Anubis features ancient Egyptian architecture.
explanation: Anubis is characterized by its setting within ancient Egyptian temples and waterways.

questionText: The Krieg 552 (SG 553) is a Terrorist-exclusive rifle.
explanation: The SG 553, also known as the Krieg, is a scoped rifle exclusively available to the Terrorist side.

questionText: In competitive play, friendly fire is always enabled.
explanation: Players can inflict damage on their teammates, including killing them, in competitive CS2 matches.

questionText: G2 Esports won the IEM Katowice 2023 tournament.
explanation: G2 Esports famously broke their long-standing trophy drought by winning IEM Katowice 2023 without dropping a single map in playoffs.

questionText: The Galil AR is a cheaper alternative to the AK-47 for the Terrorist side.
explanation: The Galil AR is a less expensive rifle option for Terrorists, often used on force buy rounds.

questionText: The sound of footsteps is distinct for enemies and teammates in CS2.
explanation: Footstep sounds are identical for both enemies and teammates; players must rely on visual cues or team communication to identify them.

questionText: Vertigo's B site is located on the lower floor relative to A site.
explanation: Both A and B sites on Vertigo are generally on the same or similar elevated levels of the skyscraper.

questionText: Sh1ro is known for his aggressive rifling style.
explanation: Dmitry 'sh1ro' Sokolov is predominantly known for his calm, passive, and precise AWPing and rifling.

questionText: The bomb defuse time is 7 seconds without a Defuse Kit.
explanation: Without a Defuse Kit, Counter-Terrorists must spend 7 seconds defusing the planted bomb.

questionText: The MP7 is available to both CT and T sides.
explanation: The MP7 is one of the few SMGs that can be purchased by both Terrorist and Counter-Terrorist teams.

questionText: In CS2, the smoke grenade now correctly interacts with volumetric light, allowing players to peek through gaps in smoke if lit from certain angles.
explanation: CS2's volumetric smoke allows light to pass through it, creating dynamic shadows and potential visual lines depending on lighting conditions.

questionText: Overpass has a 'Water' callout near the B bomb site.
explanation: The area around the B site on Overpass is famously referred to as 'Water' due to the canal running through it.

questionText: The MAG-7 is a CT-exclusive shotgun.
explanation: The MAG-7 is a pump-action shotgun that can only be purchased by the Counter-Terrorist team.

questionText: The minimum number of rounds needed to win a half in competitive CS2 is 13.
explanation: A team must win 16 rounds to win a match in regulation, and the first half ends after 15 rounds, not when a team reaches 13 wins.

questionText: The 'run boost' mechanic was removed in CS2.
explanation: While some physics were tweaked, run boosting (jumping on a teammate's head for extra height/speed) is still possible in CS2.

questionText: Mirage's 'Window' callout provides a direct view into the A bomb site from Mid.
explanation: The Window position in Mid offers a crucial sightline directly into the A bomb site on Mirage.

questionText: The R8 Revolver can be purchased by both CT and T sides.
explanation: The R8 Revolver is a powerful pistol option available to both Terrorist and Counter-Terrorist teams.

questionText: The maximum number of Molotovs/Incendiary Grenades a player can carry is one.
explanation: Players are limited to carrying only one Molotov (T-side) or Incendiary Grenade (CT-side) at a time.

questionText: The SG 553 (Krieg 552) has a scope.
explanation: The SG 553 is a scoped rifle, providing increased accuracy at longer ranges when zoomed in.

questionText: In CS2, a successful grenade throw will always produce a visual trajectory line for the thrower.
explanation: Grenade trajectory lines are only visible in practice modes or with specific server commands, not in standard competitive play.

questionText: The monetary reward for an individual kill with an AWP is $100.
explanation: Despite its high cost and power, the AWP has one of the lowest kill rewards in the game, granting only $100 per kill.

questionText: The weapon switch speed in CS2 is identical to CS:GO.
explanation: CS2 introduced slightly faster weapon switch animations and speeds compared to CS:GO.

questionText: The MP5-SD is a CT-exclusive SMG.
explanation: The MP5-SD, a silenced SMG, is available for purchase by both Terrorist and Counter-Terrorist teams.

questionText: The bomb timer in CS2 is 40 seconds.
explanation: Once planted, the bomb will explode after 40 seconds if not defused.

questionText: The M4A4 kill reward is $300.
explanation: Like most standard rifles, the M4A4 grants a $300 kill reward.

questionText: The P90 kill reward is $300.
explanation: The P90, classified as an SMG, gives a $600 kill reward, double that of rifles.

questionText: The Stinger is a common callout on the map Inferno.
explanation: Stinger is a callout typically found on the map Nuke, referring to a position near the A site.

questionText: In CS2, water no longer dampens bullet penetration.
explanation: Water still significantly reduces bullet penetration and damage, making it harder to shoot through.

questionText: The Great Danes refers to the legendary Astralis lineup that dominated CS:GO.
explanation: The Astralis core of device, dupreeh, Xyp9x, gla1ve, and Magisk, all Danish players, were famously dubbed 'The Great Danes'.

questionText: The XM1014 shotgun has a higher kill reward than the Nova shotgun.
explanation: All shotguns in CS2 (Nova, XM1014, MAG-7, Sawed-Off) provide a $900 kill reward.

questionText: The FAMAS is a CT-exclusive rifle costing $2050.
explanation: The FAMAS is a Counter-Terrorist rifle and is priced at $2050, making it a common force-buy option.

questionText: The maximum number of HE Grenades a player can carry is two.
explanation: Players are limited to carrying only one High Explosive (HE) Grenade at a time.

questionText: In CS2, bullet impacts are client-side only and not networked.
explanation: While there's client-side prediction, bullet impacts and hit registration are ultimately networked and validated by the server to ensure accuracy.

questionText: The first CS:GO Major was ESL One Cologne 2014.
explanation: The first CS:GO Major was DreamHack Winter 2013, held in Jönköping, Sweden.

questionText: The P2000 is a CT-exclusive pistol with 13 rounds per magazine.
explanation: The P2000 is a Counter-Terrorist starting pistol that holds 13 rounds in its magazine.

questionText: The 'peekers advantage' was completely eliminated with the move to CS2's sub-tick system.
explanation: While CS2's sub-tick system aims to reduce it, 'peekers advantage' is an inherent aspect of online gaming due to network latency and cannot be completely eliminated.

questionText: The Glock-18 has 20 rounds per magazine.
explanation: The Glock-18, the Terrorist starting pistol, has a magazine capacity of 20 rounds.

questionText: The map Dust II's 'Mid Doors' area allows visibility directly into the CT spawn.
explanation: Mid Doors on Dust II offers visibility into the platform and tunnels, but not directly into the CT spawn itself.

questionText: In CS2, the smoke grenade's visual effect is now volumetric, meaning it casts dynamic shadows and interacts with light.
explanation: CS2's smokes are volumetric objects that dynamically interact with the environment, casting shadows and reacting to light sources.

questionText: The monetary reward for an individual kill with a P250 is $300.
explanation: The P250, like most pistols, grants a standard $300 kill reward.

questionText: The AWP can penetrate all walls on the map Nuke.
explanation: While the AWP has high penetration, many thick walls and structural elements on Nuke are impenetrable to its bullets.

questionText: Team Liquid won the Intel Grand Slam Season 2.
explanation: Team Liquid famously won the Intel Grand Slam Season 2 in 2019, securing four major tournament wins in record time.

questionText: The Terrorist side starts with $800 in the first round of a half.
explanation: Both teams start with $800 at the beginning of the first round of each half (Round 1 and Round 16).

questionText: The CZ75-Auto is a full-auto pistol available to both sides.
explanation: The CZ75-Auto is a unique pistol option for both teams, offering a full-automatic firing mode with limited ammunition.

questionText: In the professional CS2 scene, does the PGL Major Copenhagen 2024 mark the first-ever Major championship held for Counter-Strike 2?
explanation: The PGL Major Copenhagen 2024 was the inaugural Major tournament for Counter-Strike 2, following the transition from CS:GO.
